self_report.correct_answer: The inequality ((2n-1)/e)^((2n-1)/2) < 1·3·5···(2n-1) < ((2n+1)/e)^((2n+1)/2) holds for all positive integers n.
self_report.viz_verdict: The two bound curves L(n) and U(n) sandwich the partial-sum values S_n = sum_{k=1}^n ln(2k-1) at every integer n checked (e.g., n=10: 18.47 < 20.30 < 21.47; n=20: 51.94 < 54.12 < 55.63), and the U−L gap is strictly positive.
self_report.notes: This is the classic Putnam 1996 B2 inequality; visual comparison of L(n), U(n) against the partial sum of ln(2k-1) confirms strict containment for all checked n.
